name: Rohinton Mistry
bio: Rohinton Mistry is an Indian-born Canadian writer. He has been the recipient of the Neustadt International Prize for Literature in 2012. Each of his three novels was shortlisted for the Booker Prize. All have been set in India, told from the perspective of Parsis, and explore themes of family life, poverty, discrimination, and the corrupting influence of society.

awards.name: Governor General's Award for English-language fiction
Honorary doctor of the University of Ottawa
Member of the Order of Canada
Fellow of the Royal Society of Literature
